references.text: Backer, CA & RC Bakhuizen van den Brink. 1963. Flora of Java 1: 147-149.
Moody, K. 1989. Weeds reported in rice in South and Southeast Asia. IRRI, Philippines

Zhang, Y, X Lu, S Zeng, X Huang, Z Guo, Y Zheng, Y Tian & B Zheng. 2015. Nutritional composition, physiological functions and processing of lotus (Nelumbo nucifera Gaertn.) seeds: a review. Phytochemistry Reviews 14: 321-334.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 December 2021

species.citation: Gaertn. , Fruct. Sem. Pl. 1 (1788) 73 ; Moody, Weeds reported in rice in South and Southeast Asia (1989) 289. = Nelumbium nelumbo (L.) Druce , Bot. Exch. Club Brit. Isles Rep. 3 (1914) 421 ; Merr., Interp. Herb. Amboin. (1917) 218; Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 143; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 140; ≡ Nymphaea nelumbo L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 511. = Nelumbium speciosum Willd. , Sp. Pl., 4th Ed. 2 (1799) 1258 ; Blanco, Fl. Filip. ed. 2 (1845) 318; Fl. Filip. ed. 3, 2 (1878) 223; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 9; S.Vidal, Phan. Cuming. Philip. (1885) 93; Rev. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 46. = Nelumbium turbinatum Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 458. = Nelumbium transversum C.Presl , Reliq. Haenk. 2 (1835) 83.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Japan, Korea, Laos, Middle East, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Vietnam, W Asia. LUZON: Cagayan (photos), MINDANAO.
species.notes: The lotus is now widely distributed in cultivation. N Luzon to Mindanao; in shallow lakes and ponds, where it sometimes grows gregariously. Possibly of prehistoric introduction into the Philippines (Zhang et al. 2015). Naturalized as a weed in rice.
